content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF SONOMA
COUNTY ADMINISTRATION CENTER
575 ADMINISTRATION DRIVE, ROOM 116A
SANTA ROSA, CALIFORNIA 95403-2881
TELEPHONE (707) 527-2421
FAX (707) 527-2624

December 13, 1991

VIA FAX
ORIGINAL TO FOLLOW BY MAIL

John Wallace, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I am writing to follow up on our conversations of December 10 and 11 and to request the written advice of the Fair Political Practices Commission on whether a county supervisor may use campaign funds to pay the costs of a holiday party for one of the county departments. The supervisor believes that the Department, Public Works, provides extraordinary service which is a great benefit to his constituents.

The Department would like to have a barbecue style party with an anticipated cost of less than $25 per person. None of the supervisor's relatives are employed in the Department, nor would any member of his family be supplying goods or services for the party.

As we briefly discussed, we believe that funding a holiday department under the circumstances would be a legitimate and permissible use of campaign funds under Government Code section 89513(f). We would like to confirm that this agrees with your interpretation of the statute.

The planned date of the party is December 24th. We would appreciate your response by December 20, if possible, so that the supervisor and the department can make appropriate arrangements.

Thank you for your help in this matter.

Very truly yours,

KATHLEEN A. LAROCQUE
Deputy County Counsel

KAL:kal
cc: Nick Esposti, County Supervisor
